body_text:
Interac e-Transfer
脗庐
Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports
1	Introduction to Interac e-Transfer
脗庐
Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports
1.1	Overview of Interac e-Transfer Limits, Settlement, Tracing, and Reports
Interac e-Transfer allows your customers to use online banking to send money via email or mobile phone number to anyone who has an account at a Canadian financial institution. Interac e-Transfer transactions are also monitored for suspicious activity related to fraud via the Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system. See the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
There are two types of Interac
e-Transfer transactions:
Interac
e-Transfer Send Money
Your customers use Interac
e-Transfer Send Money to transfer funds from one Canadian financial institution to another. A customer initiates the Send Money transaction in online banking. The payee is notified of the Interac e-Transfer via email or SMS. A Payee who has registered for Autodeposit will have the funds automatically deposited into their account. Alternatively, payees can receive Interac e芒聙聭Transfer transactions based on account number. In this case, the payee provides their account number and their financial institution芒聙聶s transit and institution numbers. With this option, the recipient芒聙聶s email or mobile phone number isn芒聙聶t required. All other payees will need to accept and deposit the funds manually. Once an Interac
e-Transfer transaction is sent or deposited, the customer芒聙聶s account is debited or credited immediately. Settlement with your financial institution occurs on the following business day.
Interac e-Transfer Request Money
Customers use Interac e-Transfer Request Money to request money from customers of participating financial institutions. A customer creates an Interac
e-Transfer Request Money transaction in online banking. Interac
notifies recipients of the Request Money transaction via email or SMS. Recipients can choose to fulfill or decline the Request Money transaction. If the transaction is fulfilled, the requested amount is automatically deposited in the account of the customer who initiated the Request Money transaction.
This Part describes Interac
e-Transfer settlement, reconciliation, reports, and tracing. Go to the Interac e-Transfer product pages on the Client Centre for technical information, implementation requirements, and other detailed information related to Interac
e-Transfer transactions.
